topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The printout lay stark white against the dark wood of the desk. My hands were shaking so badly I had to grip the edges of the paper to keep them steady. The results. The confirmation. A cold dread pooled in my stomach, tightening with every slow breath. I stared at the percentages, the genetic markers, a bewildering map of a life I never knew. Every familiar face in my memory suddenly felt superimposed over this alien data. I kept rereading the section detailing the biological match, my eyes blurring slightly. Why now? What did this mean for the quiet, comfortable narrative I had built around myself? A sudden, sharp need to flee the room overwhelmed me, and I pushed my chair back with a scrape that sounded deafening in the silence.

[story 2]

I sat on the edge of the sofa, my knees drawn up to my chest, trying to make myself small. The results had landed like a physical blow, shattering the fragile sense of self I’d held onto. My palms were slick with sweat, and I found myself obsessively tracing the lines of the report with a trembling finger. The air in the living room felt thick, suffocating. Every instinct screamed at me to call someone, to demand an explanation, but my throat constricted. I kept swallowing hard, the dryness in my mouth making the act painful. I looked toward the window, watching the streetlights blur, wishing I could dissolve into the night, escape the scrutiny of this undeniable truth.

[story 3]

The fluorescent light in the clinic room seemed to hum with an oppressive energy. When the doctor finally laid the final report on the counter, the world tilted slightly. I couldn't focus on the neat script; my vision kept jumping back and forth. I watched the ink bleed into the paper, a chaotic mess of information that rewired my understanding of my own history. A deep, hollow ache settled in my chest, heavy and undeniable. I picked up the paper, my fingers stiff and clumsy, and felt a sudden, desperate urge to crumple it into a tight ball, to destroy the evidence before I could process the implications of being fundamentally rewritten.
